_comment: Canonical risk limits for the Growth book — the single source of truth for the deterministic reference-weight math (collector `_build_reference_weights`), the prompt's hard guardrails, and the trade-validation layer. Mirrors growth_strategy_spec_v1.md §3/§8. Keep spec, prompt, and this file in agreement. All percentages are of EQUITY unless the key says `_of_core`.
_active_quadrant_ceiling_note: Confirmed by account holder 2026-06-30: 90% of CORE (not the spec's 80% default). The living-hedge rule — the active quadrant takes at most this share of the core block; the remaining ~10% stays spread across the other quadrants at/near the sleeve floor so a wrong call always leaves a residual hedge (spec §0.2, §3, §8). Flex and cash are SEPARATE sleeves outside the core block (see ceiling base decision).
_sleeve_floor_note: 0.1% / >=1-share floor on every core sleeve — the primary risk control (spec §0.2). Never zero. The 1-share minimum is the binding floor for higher-priced names (Phase 1 is integer-shares-only).
_single_name_cap_note: Flex single-name cap 3-4% (spec §8); ~15% soft cap on any one name. Core ETF sleeves are governed by the quadrant targets + ceiling, not a single-name cap.
_cash_sleeve_note: Cash sleeve = literal cash + SGOV, held to 5-15% of equity; shock_level 3 may lift the ceiling toward 25% temporarily (spec §7, prompt Cash sleeve). This is a SEPARATE sleeve outside the core block. Because the sleeve = SGOV + literal cash, a literal-cash -> SGOV conversion does NOT change the sleeve total (a pure composition swap): the Tier-1 validator (shared/trade_validation.py) therefore EXEMPTS a SGOV buy from SGOV's per-name window when it is funded purely from pre-trade literal cash (not same-day sell proceeds) down to the literal_cash_target_pct buffer, clamping to that budget rather than rejecting. This fixes the 2026-07-09 idle-cash pathology (SGOV 28.44% vs 28.50% window ceiling rejected a $4k cash->SGOV swap, stranding ~5% of equity in cash).
_flex_sleeve_note: Flex aggregate <= ~15% until it clears its hurdle in paper, hard cap <= 25% (spec §7). Enforced by the flex engine; a SEPARATE sleeve outside the core block.
_exempt_holds_note: Permanent conviction holds — never trimmed below current weight / never forced down by the reference-weight math (balance-sheet quality + recurring cash flow give them a partial defensive role across the cycle).
_conviction_ladder_note: Risk Score -> active-quadrant target as a share of CORE (the midpoint/representative of each prompt band, made into a number so the LLM executes toward it rather than interpreting a prose range). Top rung is capped at the 90% ceiling. Monotonic: lower risk score (higher conviction) concentrates harder. At 9-10 (no read) the book is capital-preservation: minimal quadrant bet, lean to ballast.
_no_read_ballast_note: In a low-conviction / no-read regime (conviction proxy >= conviction_score_min, i.e. the 'low' and 'no_read' ladder rungs), the spec §Calculated-Risk-Score says: overweight GLD + long-duration Treasuries (TLT), push the cash sleeve toward its ceiling, minimal quadrant bet. So the reference routes ballast_target_pct_of_core to the ballast_names (split equally) instead of leaving the tiny active-quadrant target to be swamped by the AMZN/GOOGL exemption on renormalize. This fixes the degenerate no-read reference (was GOOGL 38% / AMZN 22%). Cash is separately pushed toward its ceiling by conviction elsewhere.
_borderline_blend_note: When an axis is flat/indeterminate or active_quadrant is unknown (or a transition_watch is active), concentrate into the INTERSECTION of the candidate quadrants (assets that work in both) at the intersection target, and hold the DIVERGENT bets at staged/partial size. Borderline must produce a specific posture, never a freeze (spec §3 living-hedge + brief Phase 1).
_override_protocol_note: Phase 4 (Tier-2). The analyzer executes toward reference_weights and may deviate only via a structured, falsifiable override record. max_magnitude_pp = the hard Tier-2 band — and per Finding 2 D1 it caps the RESIDUAL an override may SHELTER, not the move: for an out-of-band sleeve, required_move_total = max(0, gap - max(allowed_residual, gap_band_pp)) MUST still trade (see reference_execution). Overrides are PER-SLEEVE (mandatory 'sleeve' field, OVERRIDE_SCHEMA_V1_1). gap_band_pp = how far a sleeve's Current may sit off Reference before the analyzer MUST either trade toward it or file an override — a 'hold' beyond this band is itself an override toward inaction and must carry a record. ASYMMETRY (spec §6): a de-risk override (toward more defense) passes with >=1 clean evidence item at full size; a re-risk override (toward more risk / less defense) needs >= re_risk_min_evidence items, else it is DOWNSIZED (magnitude halved), and with no evidence it is rejected. Structural gates (falsifier + falsifier_date + clean_data_only + within band + valid direction + sleeve) apply to both. An override never loosens the deployment gate.
_reference_execution_note: Finding 2 (D2/D3) — kills the silent-hold gap. D2 tranche rule: per out-of-band sleeve per session, required_move_today = min(required_move_total, tranche_pp_max); a trade moving >= required_move_today toward reference is CONFIRMING (progress at tranche pace is first-class, the residual needs NO override). D3 enforcement: when the model's trades fall short of required_move_today AND the corrective move is DE-RISK (selling an overweight Amplifier name / buying an underweight Damper or SGOV name per shared/quadrants.py), the analyzer synthesizes the shortfall as a source='band_enforcement' trade; re-risk shortfalls are NEVER synthesized, only flagged (spec §6 asymmetry — quick to reduce risk deterministically, deliberate to add it). Synthesized trades respect integer shares, min_notional_usd, sells-before-buys, cash-after-sells, the deployment gate, EXEMPT_HOLDS, and the per-session enforcement_turnover_max_pct of equity. enforce=false disables synthesis (shortfalls are then only flagged).
_policy_axis_note: FOLLOWUPS #16 — the resolved policy leg of the classifier (collector _build_policy_axis). LAYERING: the manually-maintained fomc-stance.json is the OVERRIDE layer — while its as_of is within manual_fresh_days it GOVERNS (a real SEP/dot-plot beats a market proxy); when it is stale or null, the MARKET-IMPLIED stance governs: DGS2 20-session delta >= +dgs2_delta_20d_bp_hawkish bp -> hawkish repricing, <= -dgs2_delta_20d_bp_dovish bp -> dovish, else neutral (DGS2-DFF spread emitted as context). 'unconfirmed' only when BOTH layers are unavailable. Gate semantics unchanged: fail-closed on hawkish; unconfirmed cannot confirm Q1.
_transition_watch_note: Realized inflation is laggy, so the Q3->Q4 (etc.) turn is called late. When the Phase-2 `leading_vs_lagging_inflation` divergence is active, transition_watch pre-stages a PARTIAL, BOUNDED lean in reference_weights toward the projected quadrant WITHOUT flipping the binding active_quadrant / regime_gate / realized inflation axis (spec §6). Convex blend fraction f: reference = (1-f)*base + f*projected. ASYMMETRY (§6 safety): a de-risk transition (projecting a MORE defensive quadrant) stages readily at staged_fraction_de_risk; a re-risk transition (projecting a MORE offensive quadrant) needs a higher bar — at least re_risk_min_confirmations leading signals confirming (breakeven AND oil, not just one) — and a smaller staged_fraction_re_risk; below the bar it does not activate. Reuses the Phase-2 divergence — never re-derives the leading/realized tension.
